description.en-US: A single disputed question of twenty-one articles on the human soul — its substantiality, its union with the body, the intellect's cognitive operations, and the post-mortem state.
description.la: Quaestio disputata una viginti unum articulorum, de anima humana — eius substantialitate, unione cum corpore, intellectus operationibus cognitivis, et statu post mortem.

sources.description: English translation by John Patrick Rowan (Herder, 1949), revised by the Aquinas Institute. Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Prologue — Prologue
Prologue — Whether the human soul can be a form and a particular thing
Prologue — Whether the human soul, so far as its act of existing is concerned, is separated from the body
Prologue — Whether there is one potential intellect, or intellective soul, for all men
Prologue — Whether it is necessary to admit that an agent intellect exists
Prologue — Whether there is one separately existing agent intellect for all men
Prologue — Whether the soul is composed of matter and form
Prologue — Whether the angel and the soul are of different species
Prologue — Whether the rational soul should be united to a body such as man possesses
Prologue — Whether the soul is united to corporeal matter through a medium
Prologue — Whether the soul exists in the whole body and in each of its parts
Prologue — Whether the rational, sensitive, and vegetative souls in man are substantially one and the same
Prologue — Whether the soul is its powers
Prologue — The distinction between the powers of the soul
Prologue — The incorruptibility of the human soul
Prologue — Whether the soul, when separated from the body, is capable of understanding
Prologue — Whether the soul, when united to the body, can understand separate substances
Prologue — Whether the soul, when separated from the body, can understand separate substances
Prologue — Whether the soul, when separated from the body, knows all natural things
Prologue — Whether the sensitive powers remain in the soul when it exists apart from the body
Prologue — Whether the soul, when separated from the body, knows singular things
Prologue — Whether the soul, when separated from the body, can suffer punishment by corporeal fire
